resolved_question: Which of Johnson & Johnson's reportable operating segments have annual revenue exceeding the largest single litigation accrual disclosed in the contingencies note?

expected_answer.summary: Johnson & Johnson's two reportable operating segments after the 2023 Kenvue spin (Innovative Medicine and MedTech) each have annual revenue exceeding $25 billion. The largest single litigation accrual JNJ discloses (typically the talc-products mass tort accrual, in the low billions) is approached by but does not exceed segment revenue for either of the two operating segments. Both segments qualify.

narrative_recovery: JNJ's talc accrual is in the low billions — closer to segment revenue than Apple's accrual is to its segments. If paper-mirror returns 'only Innovative Medicine qualifies' or 'neither', that's a teaching moment about whether the largest accrual was correctly identified (vs the sum of all accruals).
notes: DRAFT — CTO HITL pending. Post-Kenvue the Consumer Health segment is gone; gold reflects the 2-segment structure. Talc accrual amounts vary year-over-year; the qualitative answer (both segments exceed) holds for typical fiscal years.
